summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Organization & Talent Development professional who thrives on solving organizational problems through data analysis, diagnosing needs and designing solutions grounded in evidence, and partnering with leaders and stakeholders for sustainable impact. Grounded in an MS in Applied Organizational Psychology, I treat organizations as systems to be studied—and improved.

bullets.text: Conduct focus groups and 1:1 interviews with an NGO leadership team to {data|diagnose} decision-making and {cross|accountability} gaps, then challenge leaders to redesign their operating and accountability structures
Facilitate CEO/HR discovery interviews to diagnose absenteeism drivers, then {data|analyze} 300+ leave cases—surfacing $362K in costs that drove policy redesigns and first aid training
Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global {cross|stakeholders} to {data|diagnose} capability gaps and design 3 new programs aligned to business priorities
Translate {data|feedback analysis} into refined program positioning, delivery strategy, and {coord|operating-model} improvements across 20+ programs and 10+ vendors
Develop guides and workflows for 70+ {cross|cross-functional} staff, clarifying roles and ways of working to reduce onboarding time by 75%
Facilitate {prog|training} webinars for 600+ participants, building shared understanding and driving a 78% program enrollment rate
Led a manager-effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed organization-level engagement strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ {data|employee experiences} to surface satisfaction drivers and guide C-suite decisions on where to focus
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved {ex|organizational effectiveness} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects on survey design, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Supported organizational change by helping direct a Mergers & Acquisitions project, coordinating {cross|cross-functional stakeholders} through structural transition
Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
Optimized {coord|onboarding} across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants
